title: Epic Beat: Onboarding automation PoC and calendar invite workflow
description:
This epic beat owns the new `feature/onboarding-automation` lane in `/home/oz/projects/2026/onboarding-automation`.

Goal: create a deterministic, reviewable onboarding automation proof of concept for PatronPro that can be presented on Monday. The first practical slice is not to replace GoHighLevel scheduling. GHL remains the source of truth for appointment bookings. The PoC should take appointment/client data from the PatronPro/GHL context, generate a client-facing onboarding meeting email with universal calendar add buttons, and give the operator a panel to preview/test the flow.

Product direction from Oscar:
- Use the universal fallback first: ICS file plus provider-specific add-to-calendar links/buttons.
- Do not make Oscar/business Google Calendar the appointment source of truth.
- Keep the implementation deterministic code only. No LLM/AI runtime for meeting creation, email generation, calendar links, or Google Meet creation.
- Python is preferred for low-volume backend/prototype pieces because teammates are more likely to maintain it; Rust can stay a later candidate if there is a clear reason.
- For the test run, use Postgres instead of Supabase, while keeping schema and migration choices Supabase-compatible.
- Datebook is acceptable as the first candidate because the repo/package is MIT-friendly; if it lacks a needed behavior, clean-room the ICS/link generator.
- Avoid making `add-to-calendar-button` a core dependency unless legal/product explicitly accepts Elastic License 2.0 restrictions.
- Keep live GHL/Supabase/PatronPro panel mutations behind explicit approval. Default all probes and PoC actions to dry-run/read-only.
- Use WSL Chrome Profile 9 only for PatronPro/GHL UI reconnaissance. Do not fall back to Windows Profile 9 or Oscar personal Chrome profiles.

RLM documents that must be read by the next agent before implementation:
1. `/mnt/rlm/knowledge/projects/patronpro-web-docs-automation/patterns/patronpro-onboarding-calendar-invite-aut-sance-2026-06-12.md`
   - Current reconnaissance signal.
   - Recommends ICS-first architecture with provider-specific links for Google Calendar, Microsoft Outlook/365, Apple/iOS Calendar, and Zoho/iCal fallback.
   - Documents read-only GHL workflow inventory and the Profile 9 blocker.
2. `/mnt/rlm/knowledge/projects/patronpro-web-docs-automation/patterns/patronpro-main-account-onboarding-appoin--booking-pattern.md`
   - Main PatronPro onboarding appointment source-of-truth pattern.
   - Location: Patron Pro `hHLZC7FaTtUINPf3cbHd`.
   - Calendar: Onboarding PatronPro `D7x8ts5xcdNOWnd6Pjlq`.
   - Assignee: Oscar Betancourt `r2NA4HiIxWRvKwzuYpzv`.
   - Create/read appointments through GHL Calendar Events API; compare instants rather than raw timezone strings.
3. `/mnt/rlm/knowledge/projects/patronpro-web-docs-automation/research/web/run/18b869ccad1ccf5a-7f0eb-6/reports/web-research-2026-06-12-patronpro-onboar-dations-manifest.md`
   - Manifest for the add-to-calendar web research run.
   - Stored final report path: `/mnt/rlm/knowledge/projects/patronpro-web-docs-automation/research/web/run/18b869ccad1ccf5a-7f0eb-6/reports/web-research-2026-06-12-patronpro-onboar--recommendations.md`.
   - Local artifact path from source run: `./dev/agents/artifacts/doc/research/web/patronpro-onboarding-email-add-to-calendar-buttons-18b869ccad1ccf5a-7f0eb-6.md`.
4. Additional RLM hits to keep in scope:
   - `projects/patronpro-web-docs-automation/gotchas/ghl-appointment-timezone-and-delete-readback-gotchas.md`.
   - `projects/patron-pro/patterns/patronpro-onboarding-automation-map-2026-06-09.md`.
   - `projects/patronpro-web-docs-automation/patterns/patronpro-liverpool-calendar-booking-rul-line-via-ghl-api.md`.

Prior bead evidence:
- `ppweb-9` is closed and records the custom onboarding appointment test/proof. It includes the corrected main-account appointment flow, appointment `Cxa6iMN4am9r1XUdJWWS`, deleted mistaken client-subaccount appointment, and the key GHL IDs.
- `ppweb-an2` is closed and records the reschedule to Friday 2026-06-12 11:00-12:00 CDMX plus evidence artifact `dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/main-onboarding-appointment-reschedule-2026-06-12-1100-cdmx.json`.
- These are evidence and discovery dependencies only. Do not reopen them unless a future agent finds a factual correction.

Known GHL workflow inventory from RLM read-only API reconnaissance, main location `hHLZC7FaTtUINPf3cbHd`:
- `1. Onboaring Link Send`, published v15, updated `2026-06-11T12:08:45.307Z`.
- `2. Onboarding Email Automation`, published v28, updated `2026-06-11T11:19:25.242Z`.
- `2.5 Onboarding Appointment Completed`, published v6, updated `2026-06-11T11:04:48.742Z`.
- `3. Onboarding Meeting Requirements Email`, published v12, updated `2026-06-11T12:05:02.971Z`.
- Public GHL workflow API only exposed metadata; triggers/actions need Profile 9 UI reconnaissance or an approved export.

Monday PoC shape:
- A local/self-hostable panel slice where the operator can select or enter onboarding meeting details, preview the outgoing email, download/open the `.ics`, and click Google/Microsoft/Apple/iOS/Zoho add-to-calendar options.
- Postgres persistence for clients, meetings, invite artifacts, send attempts, and audit/readback state.
- A clear path to later Supabase migration: portable SQL migrations, predictable env names, no provider-specific lock-in in the core data model.
- A deterministic Google Meet/Calendar API research/prototype track, explicitly separate from the universal ICS fallback.
- A GitHub strategy track for forking/copying Carlos Anton's app into Oscar/Mensajero Caos experimentation space without touching Carlos' main branch.

Quality-loop expectation for this epic:
- Each child beat needs source evidence, acceptance criteria, dry-run behavior, failure modes, and a verification artifact.
- Before implementation, read the relevant RLM documents and official provider docs for the provider-specific slice.
- Before Monday presentation, produce a short demo script, list known limitations, and make clear which actions are dry-run versus live.
acceptance_criteria: A Monday-ready onboarding automation plan and PoC lane exists with child beats for read-only GHL workflow documentation, universal calendar invite generation, deterministic Google Meet API research, Postgres-first panel implementation, GitHub fork strategy, and quality/presentation gates; all source RLM documents and prior bead evidence are named in the epic so the next agent can continue without rediscovery.
close_reason: Resolved onboarding automation epic: children ppweb-0ka.1 through ppweb-0ka.8 are closed. Delivered read-only GHL workflow map, universal calendar invite generator, dry-run panel, Google Meet dry-run prototype, Postgres/Supabase-compatible persistence contract, approved mensajerokaos fork lane, GHL source-of-truth appointment import contract, and Monday presentation packet. Verification includes focused/broader Bun tests, targeted lint, validators/static checks, fork verification, RLM checkpoints, and pushed commits. No live GHL/Google/email/database/browser/panel mutation was performed in the PoC slices; approved live external mutation was limited to the GitHub fork creation.

title: Build universal onboarding calendar invite generator
description:
Start with the universal fallback. GHL remains the appointment source of truth. This generator should receive meeting details from the PatronPro/GHL appointment context and produce add-to-calendar outputs for a client email/panel preview.

Candidate implementation:
- Prefer `datebook` as the first dependency candidate because the GitHub/npm package is MIT-friendly and covers ICS plus common provider URL generation.
- If Datebook does not handle a required provider, reminder, timezone, or URL shape cleanly, write a clean-room generator for that part using documented provider URL formats and RFC 5545-compatible ICS output.
- Do not make `add-to-calendar-button` a core dependency unless legal/product accepts Elastic License 2.0 restrictions.

Minimum payload:
- client/contact identity
- meeting title
- start/end timezone-aware timestamps
- description/body
- location/join URL
- organizer/reply-to if available
- reminders: 1 day, 1 hour, 15 minutes

Verification:
- Unit tests for generated URLs and ICS text.
- Snapshot/sample artifacts for one onboarding test meeting.
- Manual smoke: Google link opens a prefilled event; ICS imports on Apple/iOS/Outlook-compatible clients; Zoho fallback path is documented.
acceptance_criteria: Deterministic generator can produce a valid ICS attachment/download plus provider-specific Google, Microsoft Outlook/365, Apple/iOS, and Zoho/iCal fallback links from one meeting payload; includes reminders for 1 day, 1 hour, and 15 minutes where the provider/ICS format supports it; includes license decision note for Datebook versus clean-room implementation.
close_reason: Implemented clean-room universal onboarding calendar invite generator after quality-loop approval: Mini 36/50, CE merge 47/50. Outputs deterministic ICS with Google, Outlook.com, Office365 links plus Apple/Zoho/ICS fallbacks and 1 day / 1 hour / 15 minute reminders. Focused tests, targeted lint, workflow-map validation, and git diff check pass. Repo-wide lint remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing app issues. No live GHL/provider mutation was performed; sample artifact uses fake data only.

title: Create Monday PoC panel for onboarding meeting invites
description:
Goal for Monday: show the team a functional operator surface, not a marketing landing page.

Expected controls:
- Client/contact selector or manual client fields.
- Meeting time, timezone, duration, title, description, and join URL fields.
- Preview of the outgoing onboarding meeting email.
- Buttons/links for Google Calendar, Microsoft Calendar/Outlook, Apple/iOS Calendar via ICS, and Zoho/iCal fallback.
- Download/open `.ics` artifact.
- Status/audit panel showing generated invite, previewed email, and dry-run/send state.

Implementation constraints:
- Deterministic code only; no LLM/AI runtime.
- Default to dry-run. Any live email send or GHL write requires explicit approval in a later bead/session.
- Use Postgres in the test run and keep schema migratable to Supabase.
- Prefer existing PatronPro app patterns after the GitHub/fork lane is clarified.
acceptance_criteria: A locally runnable/self-hostable PoC panel exists for Monday that can enter or load onboarding meeting data, preview the email, generate add-to-calendar buttons/ICS, persist a record in Postgres, and clearly separates dry-run preview from any live send or GHL mutation.
close_reason: Implemented Monday PoC onboarding invite panel after quality-loop approval: Mini 37/50, CE merge 46/50. Added protected /panel/onboarding-invites operator surface, deterministic preview helper, dry-run API route, Postgres/Supabase-compatible migration contract artifact, focused preview/API tests, and panel nav entry. Verification passed: 20 focused Bun tests, targeted lint, git diff check, and authenticated Playwright desktop/mobile smoke. No live email, GHL, Supabase, provider, or Postgres mutation was performed; persistence route returns dry-run statuses only.

title: Research and prototype Google Meet creation through Google Calendar API
description:
This is the RLM/docs search Oscar explicitly called out as missing.

Research prompt seed:
- `PatronPro Google Meet creation Google Calendar API events.insert conferenceData createRequest OAuth Python deterministic onboarding automation`

Known starting point from official Google Calendar API docs/Context7 lookup:
- Use Calendar API `events.insert` against `POST https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/{calendarId}/events`.
- Set query parameter `conferenceDataVersion=1` to allow Google Meet conference creation.
- Include `conferenceData.createRequest` with a unique request id.
- Use `sendUpdates` intentionally (`all`, `externalOnly`, or `none`) depending on whether attendees should receive Google-generated notifications.
- Relevant scopes may include `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar` or narrower event scopes, subject to final auth design.

Product constraint:
- This is separate from the universal fallback. We do not want Oscar's business Google Calendar to become the appointment source of truth. This track is for future organizer-owned Google Meet creation/sync if product approves the OAuth model.

Deliverable:
- RLM-backed research note with exact source paths.
- Python-first PoC plan or script skeleton, low-volume friendly.
- OAuth/Google Cloud Console checklist for creating credentials without over-broad scopes.
acceptance_criteria: Next agent performs focused RLM prompt-first/search plus official Google Calendar API docs review, then documents exact OAuth scopes, Google Cloud Console setup, events.insert payload, conferenceDataVersion=1 behavior, reminder fields, sendUpdates behavior, and a deterministic Python-first PoC plan or script skeleton; no runtime LLM dependency.
close_reason: Completed Google Meet creation research/prototype after quality-loop approval: Mini 38/50, CE merge 48/50. Added source-cited Google Calendar API research note, improved PRD, dry-run-only Python events.insert/Meet payload builder, redacted sample output, and validator covering schema and negative safety cases. Verification passed: python3 py_compile, validator script, sample JSON parse, redaction check, git diff check. No live Google, GHL, Supabase, email, PatronPro panel, or calendar mutation was performed.

title: Design Postgres-first persistence with Supabase migration path
description:
Oscar wants the test run on Postgres rather than Supabase, but migratable.

Design requirements:
- Keep SQL portable for Supabase/Postgres.
- Avoid Supabase-specific auth/storage assumptions in the first PoC schema.
- Track event source (`ghl`, `manual`, `google-calendar-api`, etc.), source ids, generated artifacts, and audit events.
- Never store secrets/tokens in normal application tables.

Candidate tables:
- `clients`
- `onboarding_meetings`
- `calendar_invites`
- `calendar_provider_links`
- `email_previews`
- `send_attempts`
- `audit_events`

Verification:
- Migration applies to a local Postgres database.
- Schema is documented enough for a future Supabase migration.
acceptance_criteria: Defines and, when implementation starts, creates portable SQL migrations for clients, onboarding meetings, invite artifacts, provider links, send attempts, and audit events; local PoC uses Postgres while staying compatible with a future Supabase deployment.
close_reason: Completed Postgres-first persistence design with Supabase migration path after quality-loop approval: Mini 35/50, CE merge 48/50. Added portable 002 onboarding persistence SQL for clients, meetings, invite artifacts, provider links, email previews, send attempts, and audit events; added migration notes and strict static validator. Verification tier: static-only because this environment has no psql, pg_isready, postgres, or pglast. Verification passed: python3 py_compile validator, strict static validator, git diff check. Existing 001 onboarding_invite_audit table remains untouched. No database, Supabase, GHL, Google, email, or panel mutation was performed.

title: Define GitHub fork and PatronPro experiment repo lane
description:
Oscar wants a copy/fork of Carlos Anton's app into his own GitHub space (`Mensajero Caos`) for PatronPro experimentation, with a clean path to later upload a branch/PR back to Carlos' repo on their timeline.

Questions to resolve before action:
- Exact source GitHub repo URL Carlos shared.
- Exact destination owner/org slug for `Mensajero Caos`.
- Whether the destination should be a GitHub fork, a new private experiment repo, or both.
- Whether this repo (`wedevio/patronpro-web`) is the Carlos app or only the current automation/docs repo.

Constraints:
- Do not change Carlos' main branch.
- Preserve upstream remote for future syncs.
- Use a dedicated feature branch for Monday PoC work.
- Consider a separate `patronpro-experiments` repo/section for exploratory automation if product wants a clean boundary.
acceptance_criteria: Clarifies the GitHub owner/org slug, fork/copy strategy, upstream/origin remote plan, branch naming, and handoff path so experimentation can happen under Oscar/Mensajero Caos without changing Carlos Anton's main branch; no fork or remote rewrite is performed until ownership and repo names are confirmed.
notes: Confirmed by user on 2026-06-12: wedevio/patronpro-web is the Carlos/source repo for the PatronPro panel/app lane, and Oscar's GitHub user/destination owner is mensajerokaos. User later approved the true fork in mensajerokaos. FSN1 alias picturelle-pro-fsn1 executed the GitHub REST fork operation, creating https://github.com/mensajerokaos/patronpro-web as a fork of wedevio/patronpro-web. REST verification confirms parent wedevio/patronpro-web, default branch main, public visibility, and FSN1 git ls-remote resolves HEAD 6a2549e5fd2e4eda250f38146158567ff0770b2d. Existing mensajerokaos/patron-pro remains a separate private repo with default branch global and was not touched. No branch push, no FSN1 working clone, no local remote rewrite, and no Carlos main-branch change were performed. Next clone/push step remains gated on an approved FSN1 filesystem path, repository-shape checks, secret scan, and explicit first-push approval.
close_reason: Resolved GitHub fork lane: quality-loop runbook approved at 48/50 after Mini rerun; user approved true fork into mensajerokaos; FSN1 created https://github.com/mensajerokaos/patronpro-web as fork of wedevio/patronpro-web and REST/ls-remote verification passed. No branch push, no FSN1 working clone, no local remote rewrite, no Carlos main change, and mensajerokaos/patron-pro was not touched.

description:

- Clarity: team can understand GHL source-of-truth, universal fallback, Google Meet future path, and Postgres/Supabase migration path.
- Actionability: next agent has exact bead IDs, RLM paths, commands, and implementation surfaces.

- Safety: no accidental live GHL mutation, no secrets, no cookies/tokens captured, and no Carlos main branch changes.

Presentation packet should cover:

- Why Datebook/MIT or clean-room is preferred.
- Why `add-to-calendar-button` is not core unless Elastic License 2.0 is accepted.
- What Google Meet API setup would require.
- Recommended branch/repo flow for Carlos/Mensajero Caos collaboration.

title: Reduce mobile LCP image payload below 1s target
description: Investigate and reduce the remaining mobile LCP/image payload after the first PatronPro website optimization pass. Current after-run shows mobile LCP 8.2s and image transfer 700.0 KiB, dominated by logo_square.png at 670.1 KiB while the mobile hero AVIF is only 29.9 KiB.
acceptance_criteria: A new Lighthouse mobile run shows materially lower LCP and documents the exact LCP element/phases; logo/image payload is reduced or removed from the critical path; report includes before/after payload table and any remaining blocker to sub-second LCP.
notes: 2026-06-12 full-auto checkpoint: generated local optimizer artifact removes the 670.1 KiB live logo_square.png critical request and replaces it with 96/192/384 PNG candidates. Live preview mobile remains performance 65, LCP 8.4s, total 1096.4 KiB, image 700.0 KiB. Local generated artifact mobile is performance 91, LCP 3.2s, total 263.3 KiB, image 47.5 KiB; image/total budgets pass but LCP still misses 2.5s current budget and 1.0s target. Evidence: dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/mobile-lcp-local-vs-live-2026-06-12.md. Leave open until optimized HTML is saved to GHL/realistic preview and mobile LCP is pushed below budget. 2026-06-15 WebP-only local preflight: generated HTML removes the 670.1 KiB PNG logo from the critical path and embeds 96/192/384 WebP logo variants (2668/7612/22078 bytes). Caveat: current live Liverpool GHL page still uses previously published PNG HTML until generation and website publication are rerun.

title: Automate website SEO social preview and Lighthouse QA
description: Make generated PatronPro landing pages include repeatable SEO, Open Graph/WhatsApp/Messenger preview, structured data, and automated Lighthouse analysis as part of the onboarding website automation template.
acceptance_criteria: Generated HTML prompt requires canonical/meta description, Open Graph/Twitter preview tags using a 1200x630 website_social_image custom value, JSON-LD LocalBusiness/Service data, service/section anchors, and no JS-based GHL custom value reads; image generation creates/syncs website_social_image; a reusable Lighthouse JSON analyzer script outputs mobile/desktop scores, LCP, payload, image opportunities, and gotchas.

title: Add ultra-small mobile responsive image variants
description: Extend the PatronPro website optimizer beyond 640/960/1440 variants. Add smaller mobile-first candidates such as 320/480/720 where appropriate, tighten picture sizes/media attributes, and verify whether the browser can choose a smaller asset than the current 960w mobile hero.
acceptance_criteria: Generated HTML offers mobile-appropriate AVIF/WebP/JPEG srcsets; mobile Lighthouse/network proof shows the selected hero and below-fold images are the intended smaller variants; desktop quality remains acceptable.
close_reason: Completed in full-auto mobile LCP pass. Optimizer now generates 320/480/720/960/1440 AVIF/WebP/JPEG page variants, media-scoped mobile/desktop picture sources and hero preloads, and compact 96/192/384 logo PNG candidates. Local generated Lighthouse/network proof at dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/lighthouse-mobile-lcp-local-generated-2026-06-12.md shows mobile selected 720 AVIF hero/about and compact logo assets; temporary server logs also showed 480 AVIF selections during the mobile pass. Desktop generated artifact remains acceptable: performance 99, LCP 0.7s.

title: Google Meet live adapter readiness and sync policy
description: Follow-up epic for moving the completed dry-run Google Meet research toward a safe live adapter. GHL remains appointment source of truth; Google Calendar event and Meet URL are derived provider artifacts. This epic starts only after product approves Google Console/OAuth setup and a target organizer/test calendar.
acceptance_criteria: Google Cloud/OAuth setup, test-calendar proof, live safety gates, idempotent event creation, update/cancel/reconcile policy, and rollback procedure are documented and verified before any customer-facing Google Meet creation is enabled.
notes: 2026-06-15 language notification update: after the user changed Liverpool Digital's Business Profile/platform language, a new PatronPro main-account appointment 3OKhJqtDyYELn0nCaxRf was created for info@picturelle.com at 16:30 CDMX with toNotify=true. GHL generated Meet URL https://meet.google.com/igh-etho-fyg, but conversation readback showed the appointment notification email remained English. Follow-up ppweb-kvi.4 tracks Spanish/English template branching on a GHL-accessible onboarding language field.

title: Prepare Google Cloud OAuth test-calendar setup for onboarding Meet adapter
description: Configure and document the Google Cloud project, Calendar API enablement, OAuth consent/branding, test users, OAuth client, redirect URIs, scopes, and dedicated test calendar needed before the PatronPro Google Meet adapter can run live.
acceptance_criteria: A setup report names the Google Cloud project, enabled API, OAuth audience, test users, authorized domains/redirect URIs, selected scopes, test calendar ID source, credential storage path policy, and confirms no secrets are committed.

title: Implement Google Calendar Meet event update cancel and reconciliation contract
description: Define and implement the Google Calendar provider sync contract for onboarding Meet events after the live adapter is approved: idempotent create, update on reschedule, cancel/delete on appointment cancellation, replacement appointment handling, retries, and manual reconciliation states.
acceptance_criteria: Contract and implementation cover one active Google event per GHL appointment, persisted requestId, update/delete behavior, sendUpdates policy, 429/5xx retry limits, 4xx no-retry behavior, Google-event-missing/local-record-missing reconciliation, rollback, and redacted audit logs.

title: Verify GHL-native Google Meet creation through meetingLocationId
description: Verify whether the PatronPro main Onboarding PatronPro calendar can create a Google Meet link directly through HighLevel appointment creation by selecting a configured Google Meet meeting location via meetingLocationId. This must run before committing to the separate Google Calendar API/OAuth fallback path.
acceptance_criteria: Report confirms calendar type, assigned user Google integration, linked Google Calendar, available calendar/team member locationConfigurations, exact Google Meet meetingLocationId, dry-run payload, approved test appointment result, whether Meet URL is visible in API/UI/notification email, rollback/delete proof, and final recommendation: GHL-native path or direct Google Calendar API fallback.
notes: 2026-06-15: Report updated at doc/reports/2026/06/patronpro-image-optimization-google-meet-change-summary-2026-06-15.md after Carlos' GHL-native meeting note. Recommendation: verify HighLevel appointment creation with calendar-derived meetingLocationId first; only start direct Google Calendar API/OAuth fallback if GHL cannot reliably create or expose the Meet link. Mini quality-loop follow-up scored the patched report 48/50; no live GHL or Google mutation performed. 2026-06-15 operator checklist added to the same report. First verify GHL-native path: organizer connects Google Calendar in HighLevel, selects linked Google Calendar, Onboarding PatronPro calendar confirms a supported calendar type/location setup, API reads calendar/team-member locationConfigurations, test appointment uses metadata-derived meetingLocationId with toNotify=false, then verify Meet URL via API/UI/email and cancel/delete test appointment. Direct Google Calendar API remains fallback only if GHL-native cannot reliably create/expose the Meet link.
close_reason: 2026-06-15 live GHL-native verification passed. Using 1Password item GHL - PatronPro - api key - MAIN, API contact lookup confirmed info@picturelle.com / rSBhh1nzHdjaRXOF3F0A in PatronPro main location hHLZC7FaTtUINPf3cbHd. Calendar D7x8ts5xcdNOWnd6Pjlq metadata exposed teamMembers.0.locationConfigurations.0 with Google conference meetingId google_conference_0. First create attempt with meetingLocationType=google_conference returned 422; retry with meetingLocationType=google and meetingLocationId=google_conference_0 succeeded. Created appointment dWSuW5JkRB7ikuLOkQ09 for 2026-06-15 16:00-17:00 CDMX, assigned to r2NA4HiIxWRvKwzuYpzv, toNotify=false. API readback exposed Meet URL in address and verified contact/calendar/location/user/time by instant. Evidence: dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/main-onboarding-appointment-create-2026-06-15-1600-cdmx.json. Appointment was intentionally not deleted because user requested a meeting link for Liverpool; this is not a disposable rollback probe.

title: Define bilingual GHL appointment notification templates
description: Follow up after the 2026-06-15 Liverpool Digital platform-language test. Changing Liverpool Digital's Business Profile/platform language did not change the PatronPro main-account appointment notification language; the appointment notification remained English. Define the Spanish/English notification pattern for onboarding appointments without relying on client subaccount platform language.
acceptance_criteria: Documents and, after approval, configures separate Spanish and English appointment confirmation/reminder templates or workflow sends; branches on a GHL-accessible onboarding language field; includes fallback for missing language; verifies client and internal notifications in both languages with redacted evidence.
notes: 2026-06-15 user confirmation: the 4:30 PM language-test appointment notifications were received, but they were not in Spanish. Treat Liverpool Digital Business Profile/platform language as insufficient for PatronPro main-account appointment notification language routing. Next work should investigate/configure separate Spanish/English templates or workflow sends keyed by a GHL-accessible onboarding language field.

title: Persist raw Lighthouse runs and enforce tighter budgets
description: The committed after-run summary did not include raw Lighthouse JSON files, so exact LCP element/phases were not available during closeout. Update the analyzer workflow to persist raw desktop/mobile Lighthouse JSON and set explicit budgets for mobile/desktop LCP, image transfer, and total transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Raw Lighthouse JSON is retained under dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/...; analyzer summary includes LCP element/phases when available; CI/manual command can fail when budgets are missed; target budget tracks sub-second LCP goal separately from current feasibility.
close_reason: Completed. lighthouse-analyze.mjs now stores raw JSON via --url, reports metrics.lcpElement, metrics.lcpPhases, and per-run budget checks, and supports --fail-on-budget. Raw live preview JSON retained under dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/lighthouse-raw-2026-06-12-mobile-lcp/; raw local generated-page JSON retained under dev/agents/artifacts/doc/test/liverpool-digital/lighthouse-raw-2026-06-12-mobile-lcp-local/. Budget enforcement verified: --fail-on-budget exits 1 on current failing runs after reporting failed checks.
